session.titleNotReady: No transcript is available for this session yet.

language.label: Language
language.hint: Language used for the Acorn interface. New translations are added gradually.

fontFamily.label: Font family
fontFamily.hint: Comma-separated stack. First family that resolves wins.

fontWeight.label: Font weight
fontWeight.hint: Weight for normal text. Effective values depend on the chosen font's available weights.

boldFontWeight.label: Bold font weight
boldFontWeight.hint: Weight used when the terminal renders bold cells.

lineHeight.label: Line height
lineHeight.hint: Cell-height multiplier. 1.00 packs rows flush; raise for more vertical breathing room. Range 1.00-2.00.

maxMountedTerminals.label: Resident terminal limit
maxMountedTerminals.hint: How many off-screen terminal views stay mounted in memory before daemon-backed sessions are detached. Visible terminals are always kept.

openLinksOn.label: Open links on
openLinksOn.hint: How to follow URLs that xterm detects in terminal output.

click.label: Click (default)
click.description: A single mouse click opens the URL in your default browser.

modifierClick.description: Hold {modifier} while clicking to open. Stops stray clicks on output containing URLs from yanking focus to the browser.

confirmRemove.label: Confirm before removing a session
confirmRemove.hint: Applies to plain sessions. Worktree-backed sessions follow the delete-worktree setting below.

autoDeleteWorktrees.label: Delete worktrees without asking
autoDeleteWorktrees.hint: When removing a session that uses an isolated or linked worktree, skip the keep/delete prompt and always delete the worktree from disk.
autoDeleteWorktrees.checkbox: Always delete worktree-backed sessions from disk

closeOnExit.label: Close tab when the process exits
closeOnExit.hint: When the session's shell or agent exits (e.g. you type `exit`), close the tab automatically instead of showing the press-Enter restart prompt. Worktree-backed sessions follow the delete-worktree setting above.

background.title: Background sessions
background.description: The acornd daemon owns long-running PTYs so terminal sessions survive Acorn restarts.

controlCli.label: Control sessions (acorn-ipc CLI)
controlCli.hint: Control sessions can dispatch commands to siblings via the acorn-ipc CLI. The CLI ships next to the app; symlink it into your $PATH to use it from any terminal.

power.title: Power
power.description: Controls how Acorn interacts with macOS sleep while it is running.

preventSleep.label: Keep this Mac awake
preventSleep.description: Prevent idle system sleep while Acorn is open. The display may still turn off, and manual sleep or lid close still wins.

refreshInterval.label: Refresh interval
refreshInterval.hint: How often the PRs and Actions tabs auto-refetch from gh. Lower values feel snappier but spend more API budget.

github.manualRefresh: Manual refresh (the icon in each tab) always works regardless of this interval.

listDensity.label: List density
listDensity.hint: Choose which optional details appear in each PR row.

showAuthorAvatars.label: Show author avatars
showAuthorAvatars.description: Render the GitHub avatar next to each PR row.

showLabels.label: Show labels
showLabels.description: Render GitHub labels next to each PR row title.

showBranches.label: Show branches
showBranches.description: Render the source and target branch names in each PR row.

showChecks.label: Show CI status
showChecks.description: Render the CI/check completion summary in each PR row.

uiScale.label: UI scale
uiScale.hint: Scales the app chrome in 5% steps. Terminal text keeps its separate size setting.

theme.label: Theme
theme.hint: Built-in palettes and valid CSS files from the themes folder.

toastPosition.label: Toast position
toastPosition.hint: Choose where transient app messages appear.

background.label: Background image
background.hint: One image can be applied to the app area, terminal area, or both.

title.label: Session title
title.hint: Which field becomes the bold first line of each sidebar session row.

name.label: Session name
name.description: The editable name you give each session (default).

metadata.label: Additional metadata
metadata.hint: Secondary line under the title. Toggle off everything for a single-line row.

status.label: Status
status.description: Idle / Running / Needs input / Failed / Completed.

icons.label: Inline icons
icons.hint: Glyphs that decorate each session row. Hide to make the list denser.

statusDot.label: Status dot
statusDot.description: Colored bullet at the row start. Redundant when Status is enabled in metadata.

agentProvider.label: Agent provider icons
agentProvider.description: Use Claude/Codex/Antigravity icons in status positions and Agents History. Turn off to show status dots and text badges.

sessionKind.label: Session kind icons
sessionKind.description: Branch glyph for isolated worktrees and bot glyph for control sessions.

hover.label: Show details on hover
hover.hint: Pop a tooltip with every field when hovering a session row, regardless of which fields the row itself shows.

statusBar.label: Status bar
statusBar.hint: Choose which optional badges show in the bottom status bar.

sessionActivity.label: Session activity
sessionActivity.description: Bell shortcut for unread session activity. The Activity tab remains available when hidden.

sessionCount.label: Session count
sessionCount.description: Total number of sessions across the active project (`sessions: N`).

activeSessionStatus.label: Active session status
activeSessionStatus.description: Lifecycle state of the active session (Idle / Running / Needs input / Failed / Completed).

githubAccount.label: GitHub account
githubAccount.description: The `gh` account used to list pull requests for the active repo.

workingDirectory.label: Working directory
workingDirectory.description: The active session's worktree path (tildified against $HOME).

agentTokenUsage.label: Agent token usage
agentTokenUsage.description: Codex reads local rate-limit events; Claude reads a token-widget-compatible statusline capture. Polls once per minute when shown.

memoryUsage.label: Memory usage
memoryUsage.description: Live memory readout for acorn and its child shells. Disabling also stops the 2-second polling loop, so it costs nothing when hidden.

command.label: Editor command
command.hint: Leave blank for the OS default. Known editor binaries only; examples: "code", "cursor --wait", "subl", "idea". The file path is appended as the last argument.
command.description: Used by the "Open in editor" right-click action on diff and staged file lists.

system.label: System notifications
system.hint: Send a macOS notification when a session changes status.

needsInput.label: Needs input
needsInput.description: Claude is waiting on the user.

history.label: Notification history
history.hint: Controls Acorn's in-app notification log. OS-delivered alerts are not cleared by these settings.

autoDeleteRead.label: Auto-delete read notifications
autoDeleteRead.description: Remove notifications from Acorn's history when their session tab is focused and they are marked read.

test.label: Test
test.hint: Verifies the OS permission and that a notification can actually appear, independent of the Enable / Trigger settings above.

result.sent: Sent. Check your notification center if it didn't appear.
result.denied: Permission denied. Allow Acorn under System Settings -> Notifications.
result.failed: Failed to send. See the console for details.

notifications.permissionHint: macOS may ask once for permission the first time a notification fires.

closeEmptyPane.label: Close empty pane
closeEmptyPane.description: Only works when the focused pane has no tabs.

intro.before: The selected agent powers acorn's AI features - currently the merge dialog's

agent.label: Agent
agent.hint: Choose which AI CLI acorn uses.

customOption.label: Custom Command
customOption.description: Legacy option. Native AI generation now requires a built-in provider.

ollamaModel.label: Ollama model
ollamaModel.hint: Passed to `ollama run <model>`. Defaults to `llama3` when blank.

llmModel.label: llm model
llmModel.hint: Passed via `llm -m <model>` (and `llm chat -m <model>` for sessions). Blank uses the llm-configured default.

customCommand.label: Custom Command
customCommand.hint: Kept for existing settings. Built-in providers are required for native AI generation.

sessionTitles.label: Session titles
sessionTitles.hint: When enabled, acorn sends transcript context from a new agent session to the selected AI CLI and replaces the default tab name with a concise title.

sessionTitlePrompt.label: Session title prompt
sessionTitlePrompt.hint: Instructions sent before the transcript context. Leave blank to use the default prompt.

agents.requirement: The selected agent's CLI must already be installed and authenticated on this machine. Surfaces a clear error when the binary is missing.

storage.title: Reclaimable cache
storage.description: Disk artifacts that acorn cannot clean up through its normal session lifecycle (e.g. files orphaned by a crash or by edits made while acorn was offline). Sessions, projects, settings, and live terminal scrollback are never touched.

scrollbackOrphans.label: Orphan terminal scrollback
scrollbackOrphans.description: ANSI scrollback files left behind by sessions that no longer exist (e.g. removed while acorn was offline, or before the prune-on-boot logic existed). Live sessions' scrollback is not touched - only the unreclaimable leftovers are surfaced.

confirm.message: This will permanently delete the cached data listed above ({size}). Continue?

experiments.warning: Unfinished features. Behavior may change between releases; the toggles themselves are stable.

stickyPrompt.label: Pin last user prompt above terminal
stickyPrompt.description: Detects the most recent claude prompt line in the rendered terminal buffer and pins it at the top of the pane so it stays in view while reading the reply. Cmd+K clears it along with the rest of the scrollback.

cjkCellWidthHeuristic.label: CJK terminal cell-width correction
cjkCellWidthHeuristic.description: Heuristic for CJK monospaced fonts (D2Coding, Sarasa Mono, etc.): when `W` and `ga` measure the same width, halves the cell width so CJK glyphs sit on the cell grid. Skipped when widths differ, so ASCII fonts with system CJK fallback are not corrected. Known limitation: tab switches can briefly show a 1-frame glyph-size flicker as xterm's resize path resets `cell.width` to the natural W advance before the addon reapplies the halved value. Off by default.

resumeModal.label: Show "Resume previous conversation" modal at cold boot
resumeModal.description: On Acorn launch, probes every persisted session for an unfinished claude or codex transcript and pops a one-shot modal when you focus the session so you can pick up where you left off. Disable to suppress the modal entirely.

about.title: About Acorn
about.description: Acorn checks for updates automatically on startup and once every 24 hours. You can also check manually below.

toast.shellEnvironmentReloaded: Shell environment reloaded. Open a new session to apply.

updateBanner.available: is available. The app will relaunch after install.

codeViewer.binaryFileBody: {bytes} bytes - not shown in the readonly viewer.
codeViewer.truncated: File truncated to 2 MB. Open externally to view the rest.

fileDropHover.previewHint: Open this file in the pane.
fileDropHover.tabHint: Open this file as a tab.

aria.newIsolatedSessionInProject: New worktree session in this project
aria.newChatSessionInProject: New chat session in this project
aria.newControlSessionInProject: New control session in this project

localTerminals.title: Instant sessions
localTerminals.empty: Double-click to start an instant session.

empty.dropTabOrDoubleClick: Drop a tab here or double-click to start a session
empty.createProject: Create a project to get started. Double-click here.

commits.selectToSeeDiff: Select a commit to see diff

history.empty: No Claude, Codex, or Antigravity sessions found for this project
history.worktreeUnavailable: That worktree is no longer available.
history.worktreeRunBody: The resumed session was started in worktree {name}.
history.trashTranscriptBody: This moves the original agent transcript JSONL to the OS Trash. Resume for this history item may stop working, but the file can usually be restored from Trash.

errors.deletedFile: File was deleted; nothing to open.
errors.notGitHubRemote: This repo's origin is not a GitHub remote.
errors.originNotGitHub: Origin remote is not a GitHub repository.

prs.reachedMax: Showing first {count}. Use search to find older PRs.

search.noGhAccessThisRepo: No logged-in gh account can access this repo.
search.reachedMax: Showing first {count}. Refine the query for older PRs.

noAccess.noGhAccountCanAccess: No logged-in gh account can access {slug}.
noAccess.withAccess: with an account that has access, or accept the invitation on github.com.

errors.noActiveSession: No active session - open a terminal first

removeSession.title: Remove session
removeSession.linkedWorktree: This session is using a linked git worktree:
removeSession.deleteWorktreeQuestion: Also delete the worktree from disk?
removeSession.dontAskAgain: Don't ask again (toggle in Settings → Sessions)

removeProjectFolder.title: Remove workspace
removeProjectFolder.canBeMovedOut: can be moved out to the project before the workspace is removed
removeProjectFolder.removeSessionsHint: You can also remove the sessions from Acorn with the workspace.
removeProjectFolder.filesWillNotBeTouched: Files and worktrees on disk will not be deleted.

projectSettings.title: Project Settings
projectSettings.pullRequestsHint: Project-specific defaults for pull request workflows.
projectSettings.generationPrompt: Prompt for generated PR titles, comments, and merge messages
projectSettings.generationPromptHint: Used when Acorn asks the selected AI CLI to generate this project's pull request title, review comment, or merge message.
projectSettings.generationPromptPlaceholder: Example: Use a standard GitHub-style pull request merge message with a Conventional Commit subject and a concise impact-focused body.
projectSettings.rememberAfterClose: Keep project settings after closing this project
projectSettings.rememberAfterCloseHint: When disabled, Acorn deletes this project's saved settings when you close the project.

newProject.title: New project
newProject.subtitle: Create a git repository and add it to Acorn
newProject.chooseLocationError: Choose a location for the new project.

validation.single_folder_name: Project name must be a single folder name.
validation.null_character: Project name cannot contain a null character.
validation.component_too_long: Project name is longer than 255 bytes, which common filesystems reject.

agentResume.title: Resume previous conversation
agentResume.bodyClaude: There's a Claude conversation that was in progress in this session. You can pick it up where you left off, or just close this dialog to start fresh.
agentResume.bodyCodex: There's a Codex conversation that was in progress in this session. You can pick it up where you left off, or just close this dialog to start fresh.
agentResume.bodyAntigravity: There's an Antigravity conversation that was in progress in this session. You can pick it up where you left off, or just close this dialog to start fresh.

controlSessionGuide.title: Control session
controlSessionGuide.subtitle: A terminal that drives other sessions in this project
controlSessionGuide.bodyIntro: Control sessions are an upcoming way to coordinate work across terminals from a single place - handy for agents and scripts that need to dispatch commands to siblings in the same project.
controlSessionGuide.pointKeystrokes: Send keystrokes to any session in this project.
controlSessionGuide.pointListSessions: List the other sessions and their status.
controlSessionGuide.pointReadOutput: Read recent output from a target session.
controlSessionGuide.createdPrefix: You just created a control session. The
controlSessionGuide.createdSuffix: CLI that drives it ships in the next update; this terminal will start behaving like a regular shell until then.

folderPermissionWarmup.title: Check folder permissions now?
folderPermissionWarmup.subtitle: Prepare macOS folder access after updates and restarts
folderPermissionWarmup.question: Do you want Acorn to check folder access before you start working?
folderPermissionWarmup.reason: After an update or restart, macOS can re-evaluate protected-folder access and ask again later. This check opens those prompts up front for Desktop, Documents, Downloads, and iCloud Drive.
folderPermissionWarmup.bodyPrompt: Acorn briefly reads these folders only to trigger macOS permission checks. No files are changed.
folderPermissionWarmup.deniedHint: If macOS has already saved a denial, it may not show the prompt again. Re-enable Acorn in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Files and Folders.

commandRun.title: Run this command?
commandRun.description: A new terminal session will open and run:
commandRun.noProjectHint: No project is registered - add a project before running this command, or use "Copy" to paste it into an existing terminal.
commandRun.noProjectError: No project available to host the new session.

mergePullRequest.methodSquashHint: Combine into one commit on the base branch.
mergePullRequest.methodMergeHint: Create a merge commit preserving history.
mergePullRequest.methodRebaseHint: Replay commits onto the base branch.
mergePullRequest.rebaseMessageLocked: Rebase replays the original commits onto the base branch - the commit messages are not customizable here.
mergePullRequest.checksFailing: {count} check(s) failing on the head ref.
mergePullRequest.checksPending: {count} check(s) still running on the head ref.
mergePullRequest.checksBlocking: Merge is blocked by branch protection until checks finish or pass.
mergePullRequest.adminMergeHint: Forces the merge by passing --admin to gh. Use only when repo policy permits and you accept overriding required checks.

stagedRevMismatch.title: Shell environment updated
stagedRevMismatch.bodyIntro: Acorn refreshed its shell-init files in this update. Background sessions started by the previous version are still running against the old environment, which can cause garbled input and prompt redraws.
stagedRevMismatch.bodyRestart: Restart now to apply the new environment. Open agent conversations (Claude / Codex) will pick up where they left off on next focus.

memoryBreakdown.title: Memory breakdown
memoryBreakdown.subtreeTooltip: Sum of this process and all descendants
memoryBreakdown.footer: Tree ordered by parent->child (DFS). Siblings sorted by subtree RSS desc. Sum-of-RSS overcounts shared memory; WebView helpers and PTY children (claude, node) included.

whatsNew.latestPublished: latest published - no public release for
whatsNew.noReleaseNotes: No release notes were published with this version.

pullRequestDetail.notGithub: Origin remote is not a GitHub repository.
pullRequestDetail.resizeHint: Drag to resize · double-click to reset
pullRequestDetail.noCommits: No commits in this pull request.
pullRequestDetail.noFileChanges: No file changes in this commit.

toasts.shellEnvReloaded: Shell environment reloaded. Open a new session to apply.

daemon.label: Daemon
daemon.hint: The acornd daemon owns long-running terminal sessions. With the daemon on, Acorn can quit and reopen without losing your PTYs. Off falls back to the legacy in-process path - sessions die with the app.
daemon.enabledDescription: Sessions persist across Acorn restarts until you explicitly quit them.
daemon.disabledDescription: Sessions are bound to this Acorn process - closing the app kills them.

status.label: Status
status.hint: Live state of the running daemon. Updates every 3 seconds while this panel is open.

controls.label: Controls
controls.hint: Restart reconnects the bridge - useful after killing the daemon from a terminal. Clear inactive removes non-live daemon metadata. Quit kills every running PTY and exits the daemon.
controls.clearInactiveTooltip: Remove every non-live session row from the daemon registry

sessions.label: Sessions
sessions.hint: Live PTYs the daemon currently tracks. Inactive daemon metadata stays hidden and can be cleared from Controls.
sessions.disabled: Daemon disabled - sessions are managed by the legacy in-process path.

actions.openTooltip: Open this session in its project tab
actions.restoreTooltip: Adopt this session back into Acorn's sidebar
actions.forgetTooltip: Remove this row from the daemon registry

metadata.orphaned: No app-side row - orphaned in daemon.

toasts.inactiveNone: No inactive daemon sessions to clear.
toasts.inactiveClearFailed: Failed to clear inactive daemon sessions:
